text: A person who is denied the privilege of driving a commercial motor vehicle in a state for any period, who is disqualified from driving a commercial motor vehicle, or who is subject to an out-of-service order shall notify the person’s employer of that fact before the end of the first business day after the date the person receives notice of that fact.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 522. Commercial Driver's Licenses
##### § 522.063 — Notification of Disqualification

A person who is denied the privilege of driving a commercial motor vehicle in a state for any period, who is disqualified from driving a commercial motor vehicle, or who is subject to an out-of-service order shall notify the person’s employer of that fact before the end of the first business day after the date the person receives notice of that fact.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: A person who is denied the privilege of driving a commercial motor vehicle in a state for any period, who is disqualified from driving a commercial motor vehicle, or who is subject to an out-of-service order shall notify the person’s employer of that fact before the end of the first business day after the date the person receives notice of that fact.
